title: trifolium kingii subsp. macilentum
intro: Trifolium kingii subsp. macilentum, also known as the rare and endemic subspecies of the King's Clover, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the pea family and is native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Trifolium kingii subsp. macilentum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
